title: ophrys cornuta var. banatica
intro: Ophrys cornuta var. banatica, also known as the Banat bee orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family. This rare and endangered plant is native to the Banat region in Romania and is known for its unique appearance, with a yellow and brown coloration resembling a bee, which in turn attracts pollinators. The Banat bee orchid has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its roots are believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas and infusions, which are said to provide health benefits such as soothing respiratory issues and reducing fever, but further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
